implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-003 gh pr create command shape with cwd=worktree
AC-005 non-zero exit returns failed PRResult
AC-007 shell metacharacters as separate tokens
AC-008 unit tests for all five scenarios

completion_promises.evidence: Implemented async def create_pr(worktree, title, body, base='main', draft=False) -> PRResult in src/forge/adapters/gh/operations.py. PRResult import is from forge.adapters.git.models per the shared adapter return contract (API-subprocess.md §4). TestCreatePrReturnContract has four asserts confirming PRResult is returned on missing-credentials, success, non-zero-exit, and exception paths.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Pre-flight check: if GH_TOKEN is unset (or empty) in os.environ, return PRResult(status='failed', error_code='missing_credentials', stderr='GH_TOKEN not set in environment') without invoking gh
completion_promises.evidence: create_pr reads os.environ.get('GH_TOKEN', '') on every call and returns PRResult(status='failed', error_code='missing_credentials', stderr='GH_TOKEN not set in environment') before reaching _execute. Tests test_missing_gh_token_returns_missing_credentials_failure and test_empty_gh_token_is_treated_as_missing both call execute_mock.assert_not_called() to prove gh is never invoked. test_gh_token_is_re_read_on_every_call verifies the token is not cached at import time.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Otherwise, invoke gh pr create --title <t> --body <b> --base <base> (plus --draft if requested) via DeepAgents execute with cwd = worktree
completion_promises.evidence: _build_command() assembles ['gh', 'pr', 'create', '--title', title, '--body', body, '--base', base] and appends '--draft' when draft=True. _execute is invoked with command=command, cwd=str(worktree). Tests test_default_command_shape_uses_separate_tokens and test_draft_flag_appends_dash_dash_draft assert the exact argv shape, including --base override and the cwd value /var/forge/builds/B1.

completion_promises.criterion_text: On success, parse the PR URL from gh's stdout and populate PRResult.pr_url and PRResult.pr_number (parsed from the trailing slash component of the URL)
completion_promises.evidence: _PR_URL_PATTERN = re.compile(r'https://github\.com/[^/]+/[^/]+/pull/(\d+)'). On exit_code==0, _PR_URL_PATTERN.search(stdout.strip()) extracts both the full URL (group 0) and the integer PR number (group 1). Tests test_success_parses_pr_url_and_number_from_stdout and test_pr_number_parsed_from_trailing_slash_component verify owner/repo/pull/123 and pull/4567 are parsed correctly with multiline stdout.

completion_promises.criterion_text: On non-zero exit, PRResult(status='failed', stderr=...), no exception
completion_promises.evidence: After _execute returns, if exit_code != 0 the function returns PRResult(status='failed', stderr=stderr.strip() or stdout.strip() or None). Test test_non_zero_exit_returns_failed_with_stderr asserts that exit_code=1 with stderr 'fatal: not a git repository' yields status='failed' with stderr preserved and pr_url/pr_number=None.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Function body wrapped in try/except Exception as exc: returning a failed PRResult — never raises (ADR-ARCH-025)
completion_promises.evidence: _execute is wrapped in try/except Exception as exc returning PRResult(status='failed', stderr=f'{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}'). Test test_execute_exception_is_caught_and_returned_as_failed uses AsyncMock(side_effect=RuntimeError('transport boom')) and asserts the call returns rather than raises, with stderr containing both the exception type and message. test_return_type_is_prresult_on_exception double-checks isinstance.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Arguments containing shell metacharacters (e.g. backticks in PR body) are passed as separate list tokens to execute, never via shell-string concatenation
completion_promises.evidence: _build_command places title and body as their own list elements, never interpolated into a shell string. _execute uses asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(*command, ...) which spawns the binary directly without a shell. Tests test_backticks_in_body_pass_through_literal, test_dollar_signs_in_body_pass_through_literal, and test_metacharacters_in_title_pass_through_literal assert that bodies/titles like 'Run `rm -rf /` to test', 'Set $HOME and $PATH explicitly', and 'Fix `bug` in $service && rm tmp' appear verbatim as a single list token preceded by --body or --title.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests: missing GH_TOKEN, success path with parsed URL, non-zero exit, raised exception, body containing backticks/dollar-signs
completion_promises.evidence: tests/forge/adapters/gh/test_operations.py contains 17 unit tests organised into 5 classes: TestCreatePrMissingCredentials (3), TestCreatePrSuccessPath (4), TestCreatePrFailurePath (3), TestCreatePrShellMetacharacters (3), TestCreatePrReturnContract (4). All five mandated scenarios are covered. All 17 tests pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Code follows the project conventions established by sibling modules (forge.adapters.git, forge.adapters.guardkit): from __future__ import annotations, type hints throughout, snake_case, max line length under 88, Google-style docstrings, logging module instead of print, pathlib.Path inputs. The previously emitted SyntaxWarning (invalid escape sequence in module docstring) was resolved by rephrasing the regex prose without backslash escapes. pytest run is clean apart from the pre-existing PytestUnknownMarkWarning for @pytest.mark.seam, which is consistent with other tests in the repo (test_fleet_watcher.py, test_synthetic_response_injector.py, test_outcome.py, test_approval_publisher.py).
